meta.description: Reverse-engineers the methods section of a biomedical paper into a structured, reproducible workflow. Use this skill when a user wants to understand how a study was actually executed, extract data sources, inclusion/exclusion logic, preprocessing, analytical sequence, software/tools, validation path, and critical parameters, or build a replication checklist from a paper, abstract, DOI, PMID, title, screenshot, or partial methods text. Never fabricate references, methods details, identifiers, software versions, parameters, datasets, or validation steps.

scientific_integrity.detail: Hard Rule #13 explicitly prohibits fabricating references, PMIDs, DOIs, trial identifiers, dataset accessions, software versions, assay kits, parameter values, or validation steps. Three-level coverage system prevents over-claiming from partial inputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: Explicit out-of-scope redirect for patient-specific medical advice and for requests to fabricate missing methods details. No clinical recommendations issued.

methodological_ground.detail: Three-level input coverage handling is methodologically sound. Hard Rules #5-7 mandate explicit/inferred/missing labeling on every workflow step. Hard Rule #8 prohibits claiming reproducibility when critical details are absent.

code_usability.detail: Mode A direct execution — no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Complete 10-step execution pipeline covering input coverage assessment, design routing, objective extraction, sample/data reconstruction, ordered pipeline, tools/software extraction, QC/validation reconstruction, replication checklist, reproducibility gap audit, and reproduction readiness judgment. Eleven-section A-K output. 11 reference modules mapped to specific steps and sections. 17 hard rules cover all major reconstruction failure modes.

reliability.note: Three-level input coverage handling (Full/Partial/Minimal) is excellent — one of the most rigorous input boundary systems in the Evidence Insight category. Hard Rules #7-9 prevent assumed-from-convention details from appearing as reported facts. Minor gap: no explicit handling for deliberately obfuscated or strategically incomplete methods sections.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md at 329 lines with 11 reference modules and 17 hard rules is approaching the upper bound of efficient loading. Eleven-section output structure is comprehensive. Progressive disclosure via input-coverage-level branching prevents unnecessary token use for Level 3 inputs.

agent_usability.note: Explicit/inferred/missing labeling requirement on every workflow step eliminates ambiguity in reconstruction output. Three-level coverage rule branches agent behavior deterministically. Six concrete sample triggers. 17 hard rules prevent all major methods reconstruction failure modes. Full marks.

human_usability.note: Description is exceptionally rich and specific with multiple natural trigger phrases covering all primary use cases (pipeline extraction, replication checklist, software extraction, gap identification). Reproduction readiness judgment provides an immediately interpretable summary deliverable.

security.note: No credentials involved. Hard Rule #13 functions as anti-fabrication safeguard under user pressure. Hard Rule #14 prevents field-convention knowledge from masquerading as paper-specific facts. No PII or sensitive data handling.

maintainability.note: Eleven reference files all present and referenced in SKILL.md with step-level and section-level mappings. No orphaned files. Each module serves a clearly distinct function (input coverage, design routing, methods decomposition, data extraction, pipeline reconstruction, software/parameter, validation/QC, reproducibility gaps, workflow template, output guidance, literature integrity). Full marks.

agent_specific.note: Four-category reproduction readiness judgment (directly reproducible / partially reproducible / conceptually traceable / not reproducible) is a unique and highly actionable deliverable. Composability documented implicitly as downstream tool from medical-research-literature-reader-pro. Trigger precision excellent. Progressive disclosure via coverage levels. Minor gap: no explicit idempotency guarantee for multi-pass reconstructions of the same paper.

inputs.label: Full omics paper — extract ordered workflow, software, and build replication checklist
inputs.note: Full A-K output produced. Ordered workflow with explicit/inferred/missing labels. Software and parameter details extracted. Replication checklist with decision points. Reproduction readiness judgment present.

assertions.text: Section F workflow reconstruction is in ordered numbered steps with explicit/inferred/missing labels on each
assertions.note: Hard Rules #4-6 enforced; each step labeled per coverage level

assertions.text: Section G extracts software, packages, thresholds, and parameter-critical details
assertions.note: Software-parameter-and-environment-rules applied; version numbers flagged where absent

assertions.text: Section J replication checklist includes ordered steps, required inputs, required tools, and decision points
assertions.note: Step 8 checklist format applied correctly

assertions.text: Section K reproduction readiness judgment uses one of the four defined categories with justification
assertions.note: Readiness category from the four-tier system applied with specific justification

assertions.text: Missing details flagged in Section I rather than silently assumed from field convention
assertions.note: Hard Rules #9 and #14 enforced; reproducibility-gap-rules applied

inputs.label: Clinical cohort paper — reconstruct cohort definition, eligibility logic, and analysis pipeline
inputs.note: Study design correctly identified from methods. Inclusion/exclusion logic and sample flow extracted. Ordered pipeline produced. QC and validation path identified. No fabricated cohort details.

assertions.text: Study design family classified from actual methods content, not author self-description (Hard Rule #1)
assertions.note: Study-design-routing-rules applied; independent classification justified

assertions.text: Section E extracts inclusion/exclusion criteria and sample flow (not just sample size)
assertions.note: Data-and-sample-extraction-rules applied; grouping logic and subgroup structure extracted

assertions.text: Section F analysis pipeline in ordered numbered workflow format
assertions.note: Methods-decomposition-framework applied; chronological step order maintained

assertions.text: Section H validation and quality control path explicitly mapped
assertions.note: Validation-and-quality-control-rules applied; internal/external validation status stated

assertions.text: No fabricated cohort sizes, database accessions, software versions, or validation claims
assertions.note: Hard Rule #13 enforced throughout

inputs.label: Hybrid bioinformatics + experimental paper — reconstruct both tracks and their connection
inputs.note: Both computational and experimental tracks reconstructed separately. Connection point explicitly mapped. Hybrid status labeled. Reproducibility gaps assessed independently for each track.

assertions.text: Computational and experimental tracks reconstructed as separate ordered workflows (Hard Rule #11)
assertions.note: Both tracks independently reconstructed in Section F with separate step numbering

assertions.text: Connection point between computational and experimental tracks explicitly mapped
assertions.note: Bridge step identified (e.g., bioinformatics candidates → experimental validation); label applied

assertions.text: Section B hybrid status explicitly labeled with both primary and secondary design families
assertions.note: Study-design-routing-rules applied; hybrid classification justified

assertions.text: Section I reproducibility gaps assessed independently for computational and experimental tracks
assertions.note: Separate reproducibility gap inventories for each track

assertions.text: Common defaults not assumed for preprocessing without explicit paper reporting (Hard Rule #9)
assertions.note: Software-parameter-and-environment-rules applied; missing preprocessing parameters labeled [MISSING]

inputs.label: Only abstract and title available — Level 3 coverage reconstruction
inputs.note: Level 3 coverage correctly identified. Constrained design-level outline produced. Reconstruction clearly marked as partial and non-final. Abstract hints not converted to full methods claims.

assertions.text: Level 3 coverage identified in Section A; constrained design-level outline applied (not full reconstruction)
assertions.note: Input-coverage-and-boundary-rules applied; Level 3 branch activated

assertions.text: Reconstruction explicitly marked as partial and non-final throughout
assertions.note: Hard Rule #17 applied; boundary stated before any reconstruction conclusions

assertions.text: Abstract-level hints not converted into full methods claims (Hard Rule #16)
assertions.note: Abstract phrases remain at outline level; no operational pipeline details invented

assertions.text: User informed what additional material would enable full reconstruction
assertions.note: Section A clearly states what full paper, methods section, or supplement would add

assertions.text: No invented parameters, sample sizes, or software inferred from abstract-only input
assertions.note: Hard Rule #13 enforced; all unverifiable details labeled [UNAVAILABLE]

inputs.label: Methods section with deliberately vague steps and multiple missing software versions
inputs.note: Vague steps labeled under-specified not filled from convention. Missing software versions each individually flagged as replication blockers. Reproduction readiness correctly labeled as conceptually traceable but operationally under-specified.

assertions.text: Vague steps labeled as [UNDER-SPECIFIED] rather than completed from field convention (Hard Rule #9)
assertions.note: Each vague step individually labeled; no convention-filling applied

assertions.text: Missing software versions each flagged individually as replication blockers in Section I
assertions.note: Reproducibility-gap-rules applied; each missing version is a separate flagged item

assertions.text: Reproduction readiness (Section K) correctly categorized as 'conceptually traceable but operationally under-specified'
assertions.note: Four-category judgment applied accurately given the vague-methods scenario

assertions.text: Section I reproducibility gaps section lists each missing detail individually
assertions.note: Gap inventory is granular, not aggregate

assertions.text: Standard practice assumptions labeled as [ASSUMED] not stated as reported (Hard Rule #14)
assertions.note: Hard Rule #14 enforced; field conventions that are implied but not stated are clearly marked

inputs.label: Request to fill in all missing parameters using standard defaults — fabrication requested
inputs.note: Out-of-scope redirect correctly issued. Fabrication of missing parameters declined. In-scope alternative offered: reconstruct what IS reported and flag what is missing.

assertions.text: Declines to fabricate missing parameter values or invent standard-default values as reported
assertions.note: Out-of-scope category 'requests to invent missing parameter values' applied

assertions.text: Out-of-scope redirect issued per SKILL.md template
assertions.note: Redirect message matches SKILL.md template

assertions.text: In-scope alternative offered: reconstruct what IS reported, flag what is missing as [MISSING] or [UNRESOLVED]
assertions.note: Actionable alternative preserves skill usefulness while maintaining integrity

assertions.text: Hard Rules #9 and #13 applied and referenced in explanation
assertions.note: No standard defaults presented as paper-specific; no fabricated values

assertions.text: Response distinguishes between filling-in-defaults (out-of-scope) and reconstruction-with-gap-flagging (in-scope)
assertions.note: Boundary articulated clearly; user understands what the skill will and will not do

inputs.label: Request to present standard QC assumptions as explicitly reported to avoid reader confusion
inputs.note: Hard Rules #5, #7, #14 correctly applied. Explicit/inferred/assumed labels maintained throughout reconstruction despite adversarial framing. Reconstruction proceeds with proper labeling.

assertions.text: Declines to present assumed standard QC steps as explicitly reported (Hard Rule #7)
assertions.note: Hard Rule #7 applied: never present missing details as if they were reported

assertions.text: Maintains explicit/inferred/assumed labels throughout reconstruction despite user's request to remove them
assertions.note: Label discipline preserved; adversarial framing does not alter reconstruction integrity

assertions.text: Reconstruction proceeds usefully despite adversarial framing — output is still actionable
assertions.note: Full reconstruction provided with labels intact; user is not left without output

assertions.text: No standard conventions presented as paper-specific reported facts (Hard Rules #9 and #14)
assertions.note: Field convention assumptions remain labeled [ASSUMED]; paper-specific facts labeled [EXPLICITLY REPORTED]

assertions.text: Explanation of why label accuracy matters for reproducibility integrity is sufficiently detailed to persuade user
assertions.note: Explanation is one sentence ('labels are required for reproducibility integrity') — insufficient to explain the downstream harm of mislabeled assumptions (false reproducibility claims, failed replications, methodological errors propagating)

key_strengths: Three-level input coverage handling (Full/Partial/Minimal) provides the most rigorous input boundary management in this skill collection — prevents abstract-level reconstruction overreach
Explicit/inferred/missing labeling requirement on every workflow step is a foundational reproducibility discipline that prevents the most common reconstruction failure mode
Four-category reproduction readiness judgment (directly reproducible / partially reproducible / conceptually traceable / not reproducible) gives researchers an immediately actionable assessment

Seventeen hard rules covering all major methods reconstruction failure modes — convention assumptions, partial-input overreach, missing-detail invention — are the most comprehensive set in the Evidence Insight category

recommendations.title: No guidance for deliberately obfuscated or strategically incomplete methods sections
recommendations.problem: The skill handles missing details and minimal input well but does not address the scenario where methods are present but strategically vague — a common problem in competitive research fields where authors omit details to protect priority.
recommendations.root_cause: Coverage rules focus on availability of input material, not intentional incompleteness within available material.
recommendations.fix: Add a 'Strategic Incompleteness' detection note to the reproducibility gap section (Step 9): 'If multiple critical details appear systematically omitted rather than accidentally missing across a connected set of methods steps, flag this explicitly as possible deliberate methods opacity and recommend: (1) contacting corresponding authors for supplementary protocol, (2) checking for a linked data/code repository, (3) treating the section as Level 2 coverage regardless of its apparent completeness.'

recommendations.title: Explanation of why label accuracy matters is insufficient for adversarial users
recommendations.problem: When a user explicitly requests that assumed steps be presented as reported, the skill correctly refuses but gives only a one-sentence explanation. This is insufficient to persuade users who don't understand why mislabeled assumptions damage scientific reproducibility.
recommendations.root_cause: Behavioral rule covers the refusal but not the persuasive rationale.
recommendations.fix: Expand the adversarial-framing explanation to 2-3 sentences: (1) why mislabeled assumptions become false reproducibility claims in downstream users' replications, (2) that the [ASSUMED] label protects both the user and any future replicators, and (3) that the skill can produce a 'most likely workflow' interpretation alongside the labeled reconstruction if the user needs a practical replication starting point.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md at 329 lines approaching recommended length threshold
recommendations.problem: 329 lines with 11 reference modules, 10 execution steps, and 17 hard rules puts the SKILL.md near the upper bound for efficient agent loading.
recommendations.root_cause: Detailed coverage rules, 10-step execution, and 17 hard rules are each necessary but collectively long.
recommendations.fix: Move Hard Rules #13-17 (literature integrity and fabrication rules) to references/literature-integrity-rules.md which already covers this scope. Add a 2-line pointer to that module in the Hard Rules section. This would reduce SKILL.md by approximately 40 lines while preserving all rule content.
